title: Beta-calibrated prior-shift adaptation by season/regime
summary: Adversarial weights already suggest train-test shift.
rationale: Adversarial weights already suggest train-test shift. Calibration literature and prior-probability-shift adjustment methods indicate that well-calibrated scores can be adapted to new class priors more safely than raw scores. This is distinct from weighting during training and can be applied selectively by regime or season.
implementation_hint: Fit OOF beta calibration on the ensemble output, then estimate shifted positive prevalence on test globally and within coarse regimes (dry/wet, phase, tyre-age bin) using an EM or prevalence-matching procedure. Apply only when the estimated shift is stable and bounded; otherwise back off to raw calibrated probabilities. Compare to plain isotonic/beta-only baselines.
expected_effect: May improve leaderboard transfer if test pit frequency or regime mix differs from train, especially when the main model is slightly miscalibrated.
risk: AUC is rank-based, so pure calibration does little unless the shift-adjustment changes ordering in the right places; unstable prevalence estimates can hurt.
